sourceTitle: How product teams boost productivity and spark new ideas with Notion AI

questions.question: What is the first step to start using Notion AI on a page?

options.text: Press the `space` key on a new line
options.explanation: The guide says navigating to a new line and pressing the space key triggers Notion AI to ask for a prompt.

options.text: Click the thumbs‑up button
options.explanation: Thumbs‑up is used to rate AI responses, not to start a new AI interaction.

options.text: Open the AI settings from the sidebar
options.explanation: While settings exist, the guide specifies the space‑key shortcut for initiating AI.

options.text: Select a block and choose “Generate summary”
options.explanation: Generating a summary is a specific AI command, not the generic way to start using AI.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a common AI use case for tech teams in the guide?

options.text: Writing better, more accessible technical docs
options.explanation: This is one of the three highlighted use cases.

options.text: Speeding up manual work and summarizing long texts
options.explanation: This is also a listed use case.

options.text: Automating code deployments to production
options.explanation: The guide does not mention automating deployments; it focuses on documentation, summarization, and brainstorming.

options.text: Broadening thinking through creative prompts
options.explanation: Creative prompting is the third use case mentioned.

questions.question: How can you improve the tone of a PRD using Notion AI?

options.text: Highlight the text and select “Change tone”
options.explanation: The guide explains that you can adjust tone with a single click via the Change tone option.

options.text: Add an AI Summary block at the top of the page
options.explanation: A Summary block creates a summary, not a tone change.

options.text: Press the thumbs‑down button on the AI response
options.explanation: Thumbs‑down rates AI output; it does not modify tone.

options.text: Create a new page and copy‑paste the PRD into it
options.explanation: Copy‑pasting does nothing to the tone automatically.

questions.question: When building an RFC template, which AI block should you insert to ensure every new RFC includes a summary?

options.text: AI → Summary block
options.explanation: The guide instructs users to add a Summary block from the AI blocks menu to embed summarization in the template.

options.text: AI → Action items block
options.explanation: Action items extract tasks, not provide a summary.

options.text: AI → Brainstorm ideas block
options.explanation: Brainstorming is for idea generation, unrelated to RFC summaries.

options.text: AI → Translate block
options.explanation: Translation changes language, not the purpose of an RFC summary.

questions.question: Which statement best captures why giving detailed prompts to Notion AI leads to better results?

options.text: Detailed prompts provide context, guiding the model toward more relevant output
options.explanation: The guide stresses that specificity and context help AI generate more accurate and useful content.

options.text: Long prompts force the AI to produce longer answers automatically
options.explanation: Length alone doesn't guarantee relevance; the AI still follows the instruction content.

options.text: Including many emojis in a prompt improves the AI’s creativity
options.explanation: Emojis are not mentioned as beneficial and may confuse the model.

options.text: Using vague language encourages the AI to fill in gaps with its own ideas
options.explanation: Vagueness can lead to off‑target results, contrary to the guide’s advice.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the thumbs‑up and thumbs‑down buttons in Notion AI?

options.text: To provide feedback that helps improve the AI’s future responses
options.explanation: The guide explains that rating AI content helps engineers refine the technology.

options.text: To automatically insert the AI’s answer into the page
options.explanation: Insertion is done by selecting the generated text, not by rating it.

options.text: To change the tone of the AI’s output
options.explanation: Tone changes are managed via the Change tone option, not via thumbs.

options.text: To switch the AI model between different languages
options.explanation: Language selection is a separate feature; thumbs are only for feedback.

questions.question: Which workflow best demonstrates using Notion AI to turn raw user research into actionable tasks?

options.text: Generate a page summary, then add an Action items block to create a checklist from the summary
options.explanation: The guide describes this exact two‑step process for extracting insights and turning them into tasks.

options.text: Highlight the research text and click “Fix spelling & grammar”, then share with the team
options.explanation: While useful for editing, this does not produce actionable items.

options.text: Insert a Summary block, then manually write a to‑do list in a separate page
options.explanation: The manual step bypasses the AI’s ability to auto‑generate action items.

options.text: Use the Translate AI block to convert the research into another language before discussing
options.explanation: Translation is unrelated to extracting tasks from research.

questions.question: If a product team wants to quickly draft a feature spec and then iterate on its tone, which sequence of Notion AI commands is most efficient?

options.text: Ask AI to write the spec, then use “Change tone” on the generated text
options.explanation: The guide notes you can draft with AI and subsequently adjust tone with a single click.

options.text: First use “Change tone”, then ask AI to write the spec
options.explanation: Changing tone before any content exists has no effect; you need text first.

options.text: Create a Summary block, then ask AI to brainstorm feature names
options.explanation: Summary blocks summarize existing content; they don’t create specs.

options.text: Insert an Action items block, then ask AI to generate a press release
options.explanation: Action items are for tasks, not for drafting specs or adjusting tone.

questions.question: Why does the guide advise against “over‑engineering” prompts when using Notion AI?

options.text: Because overly complex prompts can waste time and defeat the purpose of saving time with AI
options.explanation: The guide warns that the point of AI is to save time, so overly detailed prompts can be counter‑productive.

options.text: Because the AI cannot handle prompts longer than 50 characters
options.explanation: There is no character limit mentioned; the issue is about efficiency, not length.

options.text: Because the AI will ignore any prompt that includes numbers
options.explanation: Numbers are not prohibited; the guide does not mention this limitation.

options.text: Because complex prompts cause the AI to switch to a different language model
options.explanation: Prompt complexity does not change the underlying model.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the training process of Notion AI as explained in the article?

options.text: It learns patterns from massive training data, validates with test data, and iterates to improve its model
options.explanation: The guide outlines a three‑step process: pattern detection, testing with known answers, and iterative improvement.

options.text: It randomly generates text until a human approves the output
options.explanation: Random generation without learning is not how the model is described.

options.text: It only uses the content on the current Notion page as its training set
options.explanation: The model uses massive external data plus page context, not just the current page for training.

options.text: It requires users to manually label each AI response for the model to improve
options.explanation: While user feedback via thumbs helps, manual labeling of every response is not required.
